text.title: typescript-strict-mode Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for adopting and sustaining TS strict mode.

rule.statement: tsconfig.compilerOptions.strict MUST be true. This enables the bundle of 7 sub-flags (strictNullChecks, noImplicitAny, …).
rule.rationale: `strict: true` is the single switch that turns on type-safety basics; flipping individual sub-flags is acceptable only as a stepping stone during migration.

rule.statement: noUncheckedIndexedAccess MUST be true. `array[i]` and `record[key]` have type `T | undefined`; consumers must handle the undefined branch.
rule.rationale: Out-of-bounds and missing-key reads are the most common silent runtime crashes in TS code. The flag turns the bug into a compile error at every read site.

rule.statement: exactOptionalPropertyTypes MUST be true. Optional property `foo?: T` accepts only `T` or omission — not `undefined` explicitly. To allow explicit undefined, declare `foo?: T | undefined`.
rule.rationale: Distinguishes "field absent" from "field present with value undefined". The semantic gap matters for serialisation, Object.keys, JSON.stringify, and database mappers.

rule.statement: The non-null assertion operator `!` is FORBIDDEN on values that can legitimately be null/undefined. Allowed only on tagged-template / hostObject reads guaranteed by the runtime contract.
rule.rationale: `!` is invisible at runtime and produces a useless TypeError at the next access. Optional chaining + nullish coalescing OR an assertion function are the correct alternatives.

rule.statement: `as T` casts on `unknown` or `any` are forbidden. Narrow with a type predicate function or validate with Zod safeParse before treating the value as T.
rule.rationale: `as T` is an unchecked lie to the compiler. Type predicates and Zod schemas put the narrowing logic in code that can be tested.

rule.statement: Every function that receives external input (HTTP body, env var via process.env, JSON.parse output, FormData) declares the parameter as `unknown` (or the equivalent Zod-inferred type). `any` at boundaries is forbidden.
rule.rationale: unknown forces a narrowing step at the entry; any propagates through the entire call graph and disables type-checking downstream.
